purpose: Calculate approved takeoff and landing performance for a generic long-range business jet for operational preflight use, using controlled aircraft-specific analytical models and data.
outcomes: Produce takeoff speeds V1, VR, and V2
Produce approach and landing reference speeds
Calculate takeoff, accelerate-stop, and landing distances
Calculate takeoff, approach, landing, and obstacle-clearance climb performance
Determine maximum allowable takeoff and landing weight
Identify the limiting performance constraint and remaining margins
Block operational acceptance for invalid or non-approved conditions
Create a traceable operational calculation record

goals.text: Confirm the aircraft can safely depart from the selected runway and conditions
Confirm the aircraft can safely land at the destination or alternate
Understand the limiting weight, distance, climb, or obstacle constraint
Compare runways, configurations, weights, and environmental scenarios
Retain an auditable record of the accepted operational calculation

useCases.text: Select the generic aircraft variant and operator configuration
Enter flight, loading, airport, runway, weather, and obstacle data
Calculate takeoff speeds, distances, climb limits, and maximum allowable weight
Calculate landing speeds, distance, climb limits, and maximum allowable weight
Compare multiple runways, intersections, configurations, weights, and weather scenarios
Revise an input and recalculate before operational acceptance
Review inputs, assumptions, warnings, margins, model version, and data version
Formally accept a valid calculation for operational use
Save, print, or export the accepted calculation record
Perform the complete workflow offline using the locally installed approved data package

scenarios.text: Wet or contaminated runway with wind and slope corrections
Obstacle-limited departure requiring a specified climb gradient
MEL- or CDL-affected aircraft configuration supported by the approved data package
Hot-and-high airport near the approved operating-envelope boundary
Flight-deck preflight calculation with no network connectivity
Required input is missing, stale, outside approved limits, or unavailable

information.text: Aircraft variant, serial applicability, operator configuration, and approved data-package version
Aircraft weight, center of gravity, flap, thrust, anti-ice, bleed, braking, MEL, and CDL configuration
Airport, runway, intersection, slope, available distances, surface condition, and contamination
Pressure altitude or pressure, temperature, wind, and manually recorded weather source time
Obstacle profile and required climb gradient
V1, VR, V2, takeoff distance, accelerate-stop distance, climb performance, limiting factor, and margins
Approach and landing speeds, landing distance, climb performance, limiting factor, and margins
Inputs, outputs, assumptions, warnings, model version, data version, user, calculation time, and acceptance time

rules.text: Calculations use the aircraft-specific approved analytical model and controlled performance data for the selected generic aircraft configuration.
First-release acceptance requires demonstrated agreement with the approved analytical model across the full supported operating envelope.
The application must not produce an operationally acceptable result when any required input is missing, stale, invalid, unavailable, or outside approved limits.
Model and performance-data updates require versioning, integrity verification, regression evidence, performance-engineering approval, and controlled release before use.
The connected service target is 99.9 percent monthly availability, while the installed offline calculation path remains available without a network.
A normal single-scenario calculation should complete within 5 seconds and a supported worst-case comparison within 15 seconds on the reference workstation.
Offline operation supports the full calculation, review, acceptance, save, print, and export workflow using the latest locally installed approved data package.

For this sample, designation as the primary operational means requires operator performance-engineering approval, chief-pilot acceptance, completed validation evidence, and any applicable authority authorization.
The first release supports the baseline generic aircraft plus operator-specific configurations delivered in a controlled configuration package.
Approved interpolation and numerical tolerances are allowed; results need not reproduce the display rounding of another publication exactly when they remain within the approved validation tolerance.
Weather and runway inputs are entered manually or imported from a signed, timestamped operator data package; the first release has no live weather or runway database connection.
An accepted calculation becomes an immutable retained operational record.
Changing an operational input after acceptance creates a new calculation that must be reviewed and accepted separately.
The application automatically identifies the most restrictive distance, climb, obstacle, weight, or configuration constraint.

externalSystems.text: Signed local aircraft model and performance-data package
Signed local operator runway and weather import package
Local PDF and JSON operational-record export

constraints.text: Sample aircraft is a generic long-range business jet with controlled variant and operator configuration data.
Performance content and operating limitations are aligned with the applicable approved flight-manual and AC 25.1581-style presentation basis for this sample.
The first release is an isolated application with no direct aircraft-system communication and no live weather or runway database connection.
The operational calculation path must remain fully usable offline.
Every accepted result must be reproducible from its retained inputs, configurations, model version, data version, warnings, user, and timestamps.

inScope: Takeoff, approach, landing, and obstacle-clearance climb performance
Wind, slope, temperature, pressure-altitude, runway-condition, and contamination corrections
Maximum allowable takeoff and landing weights
Runway, configuration, weight, and environmental comparison
Offline calculation and auditable operational records
outOfScope: Aircraft families other than the generic sample aircraft family

acceptanceCases.description: Calculate a normal dry-runway takeoff for a supported aircraft configuration.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The application returns speeds, distances, climb results, limiting condition, margins, and a reviewable input summary within 5 seconds.

acceptanceCases.description: Calculate a wet-runway landing with wind and slope corrections.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The application returns corrected landing speeds and distance, identifies the limiting condition, and shows all applied assumptions.

acceptanceCases.description: Run the approved validation set across the supported operating envelope.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Every result agrees with the approved analytical model within the approved numerical tolerance.

acceptanceCases.description: Enter a weight or environmental condition outside the approved envelope.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The application clearly identifies the invalid input and blocks operational acceptance.

acceptanceCases.description: Attempt acceptance with stale or incomplete required weather or runway data.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The application blocks acceptance and identifies what must be refreshed or completed.

acceptanceCases.description: Install a newly released signed model and data package.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The application verifies integrity and approval metadata, retains the previous version for record reproducibility, and uses the new version only after controlled activation.

acceptanceCases.description: Complete and accept a representative calculation with all network connections unavailable.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: Calculation, review, acceptance, save, print, and export all remain available using the approved local data package.

acceptanceCases.description: Accept a calculation and later inspect its retained record.
acceptanceCases.expectedOutcome: The record contains the complete inputs, outputs, warnings, assumptions, versions, user, calculation time, and acceptance time and cannot be silently changed.

sources.text: Imported product interview response from the previous workflow pass
Assumed approved analytical model and controlled data package for a generic long-range business jet
Applicable approved flight-manual performance basis and AC 25.1581-style operating-information guidance
